mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns during easing cycles, or 5-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.4% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output capacity and revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 confirm robust economic momentum and corporate earnings resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in greater than 60% of months where PAYEMS rises above 200,000, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with elevated price levels creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation is low, or 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent industrial production and unemployment trends signal improving labor utilization and output efficiency, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive accuracy during peak-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference from baseline when triggers align.
